name: Scrapper Blackwell
abstract: Francis Hillman "Scrapper" Blackwell (February 21, 1903 – October 7, 1962) was an American blues guitarist and singer, best known as half of the guitar-piano duo he formed with Leroy Carr in the late 1920s and early 1930s. He was a 2024 inductee to the Blues Hall of Fame.

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Leroy Bunn
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Sort of like -- did you ever hear of Leroy Carr and Scrapper Blackwell? [INTERVIEWEE] No.

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: But you must remember that Lonny Johnson was a big star then, along with Leroy Carr, ScrappER Blackwell, and those peoples... you got the Scrap' Wells,* you got these peoples that come around to entertain these people.
